title: Office Machine Operators, Except Computer
description: Operate one or more of a variety of office machines, such as photocopying, photographic, and duplicating machines, or other office machines.

tasks.text: Read job orders to determine the type of work to be done, the quantities to be produced, and the materials needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place original copies in feed trays, feed originals into feed rolls, or position originals on tables beneath camera lenses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sort, assemble, and proof completed work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate office machines such as high speed business photocopiers, readers, scanners, addressing machines, stencil-cutting machines, microfilm readers or printers, folding and inserting machines, bursters, and binder machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Complete records of production, including work volumes and outputs, materials used, and any backlogs.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Compute prices for services and receive payment, or provide supervisors with billing information.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Set up and adjust machines, regulating factors such as speed, ink flow, focus, and number of copies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load machines with materials such as blank paper or film.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor machine operation, and make adjustments as necessary to ensure proper operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Clean machines, perform minor repairs, and report major repair needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate auxiliary machines such as collators, pad and tablet making machines, staplers, and paper punching, folding, cutting, and perforating machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain stock of supplies, and requisition any needed items.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare and process papers for use in scanning, microfilming, and microfiche.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean and file master copies or plates.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut copies apart and write identifying information, such as page numbers or titles, on copies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Move heat units and clamping frames over screen beds to form Braille impressions on pages, raising frames to release individual copies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
